text: Low energy availability arises when insufficient calories are consumed to support exercise energy expenditure, resulting in compromised physiological processes including menstrual irregularities, gastrointestinal and cardiovascular dysfunction, and compromised bone health.

Longstanding low energy availability can impair sporting performance through its effects on menstrual/libido dysfunction, gastrointestinal issues, cardiovascular dysfunction, and compromised bone health.
Methods to assess energy availability remain a challenge and contribute to methodological difficulties in identifying true low energy availability in athletes.
The Low Energy Availability in Females Questionnaire (LEAF-Q) is a validated screening tool that has shown promise in identifying endurance athletes at risk of low energy availability.
Knowledge of RED-S and its potential implications for performance is low among both coaches and athletes.

Within-day energy deficiency (prolonged periods of energy deficit within a single day) is a concern distinct from overall daily energy balance and may independently contribute to low energy availability consequences.
Sport-specific and gender-specific screening tools are needed to identify both adolescent and senior athletes at risk of RED-S across different sports.
